title: Yangtze Three Gorges Boat Rental Guide: Tips, Costs & 2026 Season
html:
Planning a yacht charter Yangtze Three Gorges lets you explore the dramatic cliffs and tranquil tributaries of China's most famous river corridor. Depart from Yichang or Wushan with a crewed boat that costs from €1,200 per day for a private group of eight. BoatHire24 connects you with fully licensed operators for a safe river journey.

Where to set off in Yangtze Three Gorges

Choose from several established departure points along the 193 km gorge system. Yichang Port, near the Three Gorges Dam, is the most common start for organised tours. Wushan, home to the Lesser Three Gorges, offers a quieter departure with direct access to scenic side canyons. Fengjie at the western end provides ancient town charm and fewer crowds. Zhongxian and Badong both have modern marinas with fuel and food supplies, while the dock at Maoping village rivals Yichang for convenience.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Yangtze Three Gorges?

For bareboat hire a Chinese navigational licence is required, but crewed charters need none. China mandates a local certificate for anyone operating a motor vessel on its inland waterways. If you lack a Chinese licence, opt for a skipper-inclusive rental your operator can provide. BoatHire24 listings clearly show licence requirements, and the operator confirms exact details before booking. The Yangtze season runs from April to October, with highest demand June through August when southerly winds are light.

Book early for July and August: these peak months see dock space fill up, especially at Wushan and Yichang, so ask your operator about early morning departures to avoid afternoon breeze.

Which boat to rent for the Yangtze Three Gorges

The right vessel depends on group size, budget and desire for comfort. Consider these options:

  1. Shared tour boat Best for solo travellers, from €50 per person for a half-day trip with up to 40 guests.
  2. Small motorboat Ideal for 4, 6 friends, from €300 for a half-day, allowing access to narrow tributaries like the Shennong Stream.
  3. Catamaran Stable and spacious for 8, 10 people, from €800 for a full day; the Sanyati model offers salon and sun deck comfort.
  4. Luxury crewed yacht Perfect for a full-day private experience with catering, from €1,500 for groups of 8, 12; check the Salty Sun Charters listing for a well-equipped 46-foot vessel with captain and hostess.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Yangtze Three Gorges?

Prices range from €50 shared to over €2,000 for a full-day crewed luxury charter. The breakdown reflects typical 2026 rates:

  • Shared tour: from €50 per person for a 3-hour sightseeing loop from Yichang.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): from €300, including a guide for the Shennong Stream tributary.
  • Catamaran full-day (8, 10 guests): from €800, with fuel and picnic lunch often included.
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day (12 guests): from €2,000, with full catering and flexible itinerary; many operators offer a discounted yacht charter Yangtze Three Gorges when booking through BoatHire24.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €2,000+ per charter, depending on vessel and duration.
  • Peak months: June, July, August (book 4, 6 weeks ahead).
  • Trip length: 3 hours (shared) to 8 hours (full-day private).
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests typical for private charters.
  • Currency: EUR (local payments accepted by operators).

Questions about boat rental in Yangtze Three Gorges

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in the Three Gorges?

Bareboat rentals are available only if you hold a valid Chinese powerboat licence, which is rarely held by international visitors, so most travellers choose the more popular crewed charter option.

What is the best time of year for a Yangtze boat trip?

The ideal time is between April and October, with June to August offering the warmest weather and calmest conditions, though May and September provide more comfortable temperatures and fewer boats on the river.

How long is a typical Three Gorges cruise from Yichang?

A full-day charter from Yichang covers about 90 nautical miles round trip to Wushan and back, passing the Xiling Gorge and the Three Gorges Dam, with several photo stops along the way.

Are meals included in the boat rental price?

Shared tours rarely include food, while private charters above €800 often provide a simple lunch; always check the listing details or ask the operator before booking.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or an experienced boater, the platform makes it easy to compare verified listings, read guest reviews, and book your Yangtze adventure with confidence. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Yangtze Three Gorges and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
